message:
Harden bootstrap dir cleanup to installer-owned paths only

\`cleanup()\` has always run \`rm -rf "$ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR"\` on exit,
which was fine for the common curl|bash path where the installer
allocates its own scratch dir under TMPDIR. It was NOT fine for any
caller that supplied \`ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR\` in the environment to
point at a directory they cared about — the installer would still
wipe it on exit (success or failure). Test rigs, CI harnesses, and
anyone trying to inspect the extracted archive after a failure all
hit this.

Tighten cleanup so it only runs when the installer is certain it
created the directory itself:

- Add three script-scoped bookkeeping vars next to the other global
  ACFS_* declarations:
  \`_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_OWNED\` (false by default),
  \`_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_CREATED\` (empty until we \`mktemp\` it), and
  \`_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_TMP_ROOT\` (the TMPDIR value at creation time,
  normalized without a trailing slash).

- Set all three in \`bootstrap_repo_archive\` immediately after the
  successful \`mktemp -d\`, so the flag is only flipped on for the
  exact directory we just created.

- Add \`acfs_bootstrap_dir_is_owned_temp\` predicate that returns
  success only when every one of the following holds:
  - The owned flag is true.
  - The argument is non-empty.
  - The recorded created path is non-empty and equals the argument
    after stripping any trailing slash.
  - The path is absolute and is not \`/\`.
  - TMP root is absolute.
  - The path matches \`"$TMP_ROOT"/acfs-bootstrap-*\` (the mktemp
    template prefix the installer uses), so a caller-supplied path
    under \`/tmp\` that happens to exist can't satisfy the check.
  - The directory still exists on disk.

- Replace the old two-step \`[[ -n ... ]] && [[ -d ... ]]\` guard in
  \`cleanup()\` with a single \`acfs_bootstrap_dir_is_owned_temp\`
  call, and pass \`-- "$ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR"\` to \`rm -rf\` so a
  dir whose name begins with \`-\` can't be mis-parsed as an option
  if anything else ever feeds this path.

- Extend \`tests/e2e/test_curlbash_bootstrap.sh\` with a post-install
  assertion that the caller-provided \`BOOTSTRAP_DIR\` still exists
  after the installer ran. If the installer ever regresses back to
  unconditional cleanup, this assertion will fail.

No behavior change on the curl|bash happy path (the installer still
allocates its own dir under TMPDIR, still wipes it on exit). The
change is invisible to normal users and only kicks in for code paths
that supply ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR externally.

files.patch:
@@ -88,6 +88,9 @@ ACFS_COMMIT_SHA_FULL=""  # Full SHA for pinning resume scripts (40 chars)
 ACFS_EARLY_CURL_ARGS=(--connect-timeout 30 --max-time 300 -fsSL)
 # Note: ACFS_HOME is set after TARGET_HOME is determined
 ACFS_LOG_DIR="/var/log/acfs"
+_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_OWNED=false
+_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_CREATED=""
+_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_TMP_ROOT=""
 # SCRIPT_DIR is empty when running via curl|bash (stdin; no file on disk)
 SCRIPT_DIR=""
 if [[ -n "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" && -f "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" ]]; then
@@ -1130,15 +1133,32 @@ _acfs_signal_handler() {
     esac
 }
 
+acfs_bootstrap_dir_is_owned_temp() {
+    local dir="${1:-}"
+    local tmp_root="${_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_TMP_ROOT:-}"
+
+    [[ "${_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_OWNED:-false}" == "true" ]] || return 1
+    [[ -n "$dir" ]] || return 1
+    [[ -n "${_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_CREATED:-}" ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$dir" == "$_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_CREATED" ]] || return 1
+
+    dir="${dir%/}"
+    [[ "$dir" == /* ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$dir" != "/" ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$tmp_root" == /* ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$dir" == "$tmp_root"/acfs-bootstrap-* ]] || return 1
+    [[ -d "$dir" ]] || return 1
+}
+
 cleanup() {
     # Capture exit code FIRST, before any other commands can overwrite $?
     local exit_code=$?
 
     # Cleanup must never abort — disable errexit for the entire function.
     set +e
 
-    if [[ -n "${ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR:-}" ]] && [[ -d "$ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR" ]]; then
-        rm -rf "$ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
+    if acfs_bootstrap_dir_is_owned_temp "${ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR:-}"; then
+        rm -rf -- "$ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
     fi
 
     if [[ -n "${ACFS_TMP_ARCHIVE:-}" ]] && [[ -f "$ACFS_TMP_ARCHIVE" ]]; then
@@ -2301,6 +2321,10 @@ bootstrap_repo_archive() {
         log_fatal "Failed to create temp dir for extraction"
     }
     ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR="$tmp_dir"
+    _ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_CREATED="$tmp_dir"
+    _ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_TMP_ROOT="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}"
+    _ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_TMP_ROOT="${_ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_TMP_ROOT%/}"
+    _ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR_OWNED=true
     # Make bootstrap dir world-readable so ubuntu user can access scripts
     "$chmod_bin" 755 "$tmp_dir"

@@ -146,6 +146,7 @@ ACFS_CI=true \
     > "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1 || true
 
 assert_ok "Install script executed without crash" test -f "$LOG_FILE"
+assert_ok "Caller-provided bootstrap dir was not treated as installer-owned cleanup" test -d "$BOOTSTRAP_DIR"
 echo ""
 
 # ────────────────────────────────────────
